i invite everyone to look up argentina’s history during its last dictatorship: the author of the eternaut and his entire family were “disappeared”. they were kidnapped and taken into clandestine torture centers, and to this day we can’t know what happened to them. his daughters were pregnant at the time. during this time, pregnant women were shown no mercy: they had their babies in the most horrendous of conditions, and their children were taken from them and given to other families, unable to ever know their true identities. it’s posible the autores grandchildren still live, unaware of their real upbringings. the “abuelas de plaza de mayo” still look for their grandchildren.

El Autor, Oesterland, fue capturado por los "Manos" (basado en la agrupación guerrillera Montoneros) lo que le llevó a un lavado de cabeza causante de que El Eternauta 2 tuviera mucha inclinación política, como si hubiera sido escrita por otra persona. Al mismo tiempo durante escribía El Eternauta 2 fue capturado por militares. Es decir, se convirtió en un Hombre-Robot a mano de los Montoneros y luego fue secuestrado por los "Ellos" a los cuales nunca se le ve la cara en la historieta y seguramente él tampoco le vio la cara antes de que lo mataran.

Si se fijan en la serie aparecen los Kukas (minuto 1:39), no son realmente malos pero tienen el cerebro lavado, una referencia futurista los seguidores ciegos del kirchnerismo (o del peronismo cuando se lanzó el comic).


La frase "y si lo que llega no es ayuda" (min 1:08) , es un mensaje libertario de no esperar que los gobiernos nos salven, ya que debemos aprender a ser fuertes por nosotros mismos.

Love to see The Eternaut! When I was a child back home in Argentina I wrote a comic called Cristian Tonio about an Alien with this ship, becoming a statue at night and disappearing during the day. I lost the story , the drawings, everything, I wrote it before I was 10 years old. It was my favorite story, and I felt compelled to write it down as if someone was telling me the story. I guess the alien got angry at me because I could hear "them" and picked it up, the words, the signs, I think the alien Cristian Tonio, stole my work. This is the thing, same as musicians, often say it was inspired, I don't know how I wrote that, I dreamt that etc etc 
Aliens hate it when we tap into their subconscious and they will do anything to destroy us. They are cowards, instead of becoming friends, they need to destroy, if there is a God they think they are God. Wrong! very , very wrong

​On a summer night in Buenos Aires, Argentina, a mysterious snowfall kills everything it touches instantly. Thousands of people become trapped in their homes, buildings, and businesses, further isolated by the lack of communication. The first question to arise: Is this a nuclear war or something even more unknowable?

To survive outside, one must wear layers of waterproof clothing and use a gas mask. Juan Salvo (Ricardo Darín) and his friends embark on a desperate struggle for survival—and to find Clara, Juan's daughter—but everything changes when they discover that the toxic storm is just the first attack by a foreign army invading Earth.​

 The production is based on the Eisner Award-winning graphic novel The Eternaut (El Eternauta), written by Héctor G. Oesterheld and illustrated by Francisco Solano López. The comics were published in installments from 1957 to 1959, then published as one volume in 2015.++++

For those who don't know the story: el eternauta is set in the 1950s and 60s in Argentina where a group of different guys in a session of truco:(an Argentine card game) are suddenly caught in a unexpected snowing and they realize that snow is toxic and if anyone touches it it kills them so they must use special anti biohazard suits. However, the snow is not the only threat: soon gigantic carnivorous insects called the cascarudos begin to attack them and other gigantic ones and then they realize it's an alien invasion and the army tries to take them down

Héctor Germán Oesterheld, renowned writer of El Eternauta, was kidnapped in April 1977 by Argentina’s military dictatorship. A member of Montoneros, Oesterheld was detained in La Plata and taken to clandestine detention centers, where he was tortured and eventually murdered, presumably in 1978. His four daughters —Estela, Diana, Marina, and Beatriz— were also kidnapped and disappeared at different times between 1976 and 1977, along with two sons-in-law and several of his grandchildren, some of whom were illegally appropriated. His story became a symbol of state terrorism and the family devastation caused by illegal repression in Argentina.
